tools.name: tcpdump
tools.description: Capture and analyze network traffic using tcpdump. To control how long the capture runs, set the 'timeout_seconds' parameter (default 60). Do NOT use -G or -W flags for duration control — they will be ignored by the timeout wrapper. You may use '-c <count>' to limit by packet count, and '-n' to disable DNS resolution.

tools.name: tshark
tools.description: Capture and analyze network traffic using tshark (Wireshark CLI). To control how long the capture runs, set the 'timeout_seconds' parameter (default 60). Do NOT use '-a duration' for duration control. You may use '-c <count>' to limit by packet count.

tools.name: netdiscover
tools.description: Active/passive ARP reconnaissance tool to discover hosts on a network. To control how long the scan runs, set the 'timeout_seconds' parameter (default 60). You may use '-r <range>' to specify a target subnet.

tools.name: arp_scan
tools.description: Send ARP packets to local network to discover alive hosts. CRITICAL: Ensure you specify a strict target like '-l' (local network) or a specific CIDR so it terminates quickly without hanging.

tools.name: nmap
tools.description: Network exploration tool and security / port scanner. CRITICAL: For broad or blind scans, you MUST use fast flags (e.g., '-F', '-T4') or strict timeouts (e.g., '--host-timeout 5m') to avoid the command taking too long and freezing the session.

tools.name: arpspoof
tools.description: Intercept traffic on a switched LAN by forging ARP replies. To control how long the attack runs, set the 'timeout_seconds' parameter (default 60).

tools.name: ettercap
tools.description: Comprehensive suite for man-in-the-middle attacks on LAN. Runs in Text-only mode (-T). To control how long the attack runs, set the 'timeout_seconds' parameter (default 60).
